title: Upgrading from 4.024 to 4.036 results in more files and hence slower C++ compilation
body:
How may we help - what is your question?
I used the same command to verilated same file using 4.024 and 4.036. However, 4.036 results in more files (*Slow.cpp) and hence slower C++ compilation (more than 1.5x slower). I didn't find useful information in changelog and not sure what has been changed.

`verilator --cc --top-module DUT --Mdir obj_dir_DUT --assert -O3 --unroll-count 1000000 --unroll-stmts 1000000 --Wno-UNOPTFLAT --Wno-UNSIGNED --Wno-WIDTH DUT.v`

The 4.036 compilation has several ***_Slow.cpp, but I only applied -O3. 4.024 doesn't have those slow files. I tried to exclude those _Slow.cpp to compile the C++ simluator, but it reports undefined symbol error which means there are probably some useful functions in those slow files.

Is it possible to get back to the same outcome as 4.024?
